data.title: Toro reports mixed Q3 results; updates FY outlook range
data.description:
• Toro press release (NYSE:TTC): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.24 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $1.13B (-2.6% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Full-year fiscal 2025 net sales and earnings per share guidance remain within previously communicated ranges.
  • For fiscal 2025, management expects total company net sales and adjusted diluted earnings per share to both be at the lower end of prior guidance ranges: net sales flat to down 3% and *adjusted diluted earnings per share of about $4.15.
  • FY revenue consensus estimated growth of -1.13% Y/Y; adjusted diluted earnings per share consensus of $4.25.

data.title: Sunoco launches $1.7B senior notes, $1B preferred offering to fund Parkland acquisition
data.description:
• Sunoco LP (NYSE:SUN) has announced two major private offerings to support its planned acquisition of Parkland Corporation.
  • The offerings include: $850M in senior notes due 2031, $850M in senior notes due 2034, and $1B in Series A Preferred Units (1M units at $1,000 each).
  • Proceeds from both offerings will help fund cash consideration and transaction costs related to the Parkland acquisition.
  • Prior to closing, funds may be used to temporarily reduce borrowings under Sunoco’s revolving credit facility.
  • None of the offerings are contingent on one another or on the completion of the Parkland deal.
  • If the Parkland acquisition is not completed by May 5, 2026, or is canceled, both the notes and preferred units will be subject to special mandatory redemption.
  • Notes will be redeemed at 100% of issue price + accrued interest.
  • Preferred units will be redeemed at $1,000 + unpaid distributions, if any.
  • Source: Press release, +1

data.title: BitFuFu expands bitcoin reserves to 1,899 BTC in August
data.description:
• BitFuFu (NASDAQ:FUFU) held 1,899 BTC in August, an increase of 115 BTC from July 31, including the purchase of 48 BTC and a net inflow of 12 BTC from customer payments.
  • Bitcoin production: 408 BTC, a 12.6% month-over-month decrease, including 353 BTC from cloud mining and 55 BTC from self-mining.
  • Self-owned hashrate grew 31.6% month-over-month to 5.0 EH/s, out of a total 35.6 EH/s under management, BitFuFu (NASDAQ:FUFU) said.
  • Average fleet efficiency improved to 17.5 J/TH.

data.title: TOYO acquires VSUN brand to drive strategic growth
data.description:
• TOYO (NASDAQ:TOYO) has acquired the VSUN brand from its sister company, Vietnam Sunergy Joint Stock Company ("VSUN Co"), a solar module provider.
  • The acquisition of VSUN brand did not involve any issuance of new equity by TOYO.
  • TOYO -0.16% premarket to $6.17.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock index futures pare some gains as private sector employment softened further
data.description:
Stock index futures pared some gains on Thursday, as investors digested some more jobs data to assess the health of the labor market. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.19%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.61%.

Job cuts climbed 39% in August, boosting the YTD total higher than all of 2024.

U.S. private sector employment continued softening, slightly more than expected, in August.

Initial jobless claims rose slightly more than expected in the past week. Productivity was revised up, and labor costs were revised lower in second Q2 prints.

The U.S. goods and services trade deficit widened more than expected in July.

Wall Street closed mostly higher on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq and S&P 500 advancing while the Dow finished near the flat line, as investor sentiment got a bit of a boost after Google avoided steep penalties in a pivotal regulatory case.

"The global bond selloff finally paused for breath yesterday, as weak U.S. data meant investors ramped up their expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts this year. The main catalyst was the JOLTS report for July, which showed that job openings fell to a 10-month low and exacerbated fears about a labor market slowdown," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

The August PMI composite final and the ISM services index will land during market hours. The former is expected to come in at 55.4 and the latter at 50.9.

"Perhaps the most interesting data today will be the prices paid components in the ISM services release.... Last month it climbed to 69.9, which, if you took at face value from the graph predicts over 5% CPI in the coming months. Now clearly that's highly unlikely, as the prices paid are more prone to spikes than CPI, but it shows where the momentum and risks are for now. So markets do need to see this mean revert lower soon," Reid said.

The Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day. 

On more of the political side, President Donald Trump on Wednesday urged the Supreme Court to quickly accept and rule on an appeal aiming to overturn lower court rulings that deemed most of his tariffs illegal.

data.title: Microsoft-backed Mistral in talks to raise €2B: report
data.description:
• French artificial intelligence company Mistral, which is partially backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), is in final talks to raise €2B that would value it at €12B, Bloomberg reported.
  • The company, which was founded in 2023, is working on an open-source language model and chatbot (similar to ChatGPT and others) known as Le Chat.
  • Mistral was previously in talks to raise more than $1B from investors, including Abu Dhabi's MGX investment fund.
  • Mistral did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • In May, G42 and Mistral AI announced a partnership to co-develop next-generation AI platforms and infrastructure. One month later, Mistral partnered with Nvidia (NVDA) to create Mistral Compute, which provides customers with their own private and integrated stack, which includes GPUs, orchestration, APIs, products and services.

data.title: Productivity revised up, labor costs revised lower in second Q2 prints
data.description: Nonfarm labor productivity climbed 3.3% at an annualized rate in Q2, exceeding the +2.8% expected and +2.4% in the preliminary estimate, according to preliminary data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

data.title: Guardant drops after data for Shield colorectal cancer screening test
data.description:
• Guardant Health (NASDAQ:GH) lost ~9% in the premarket on Thursday after the company shared data from a clinical trial for its latest colorectal cancer (CRC) screening algorithm (V2) used in its Shield blood test.
  • The study, an extension of Guardant’s registrational study ECLIPSE, met all primary endpoints, the company said, adding the screening algorithm helped detect 84% of cancers accurately (sensitivity) and correctly returned a negative result 90% of the time (specificity).
  • Regarding stage I CRC, the V2 algorithm indicated 62% specificity, Guardant (NASDAQ:GH) added. Exact Sciences (NASDAQ:EXAS), the maker of stool-based colorectal cancer screening test Cologuard, added ~3% after the readout.
  • ECLIPSE data, published in The New England Journal of Medicine, indicated 83% sensitivity for colorectal cancer, one of the co-primary endpoints of the trial, which was designed to test Shield in CRC detection among average-risk adults.

data.title: Atlassian to acquire The Browser Company
data.description:
• Atlassian (NASDAQ:TEAM) said on Thursday that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire The Browser Company, the company behind the Dia and Arc browsers. 
  • Under the terms of the definitive agreement, Atlassian will acquire The Browser Company for approximately $610M in cash, inclusive of The Browser Company’s cash balance. 
  • The transaction will be funded through cash from Atlassian’s balance sheet and is expected to close in the second quarter of its fiscal year 2026. 
  • The acquisition is not expected to have a material impact on the company’s financials in fiscal years 2026 and 2027.

data.title: Williams-Sonoma may have an edge on peers in implementing AI across its business
data.description:
Williams-Sonoma (NYSE:WSM) is emerging as one of the AI application leaders in the retail industry.

Notably, Williams-Sonoma (NYSE:WSM) has expanded its AI-powered customer service assistant across all of its brands, updated Jefferies analyst Corey Tarlowe. He said the retailer is aiming to improve resolution speed and reduce service costs. In addition, the company also previewed an upcoming AI culinary companion tool designed to improve product discovery and offer entertaining tips. Management discussed using AI to deepen personalization across its multichannel customer experience while leveraging data to improve issue resolution and reduce operating costs.

During Williams-Sonoma's (WSM) second-quarter earnings call, the topic of AI was front and center. The expectation is that there will be further opportunity for supply chain efficiencies in the long term as AI capabilities are implemented.

"We don't view AI as a standalone function," highlighted CEO Laura Alber. "It is embedded across our business," she added. The company has AI organized to enhance the customer experience, optimize the supply chain, and automate internal operations. 

"On the customer experience, we are already seeing strong results from our new AI-powered customer service assistant, which we launched with Pottery Barn Kids earlier this summer and are scaling across all of our brands this week. It is improving issue resolution rates and speed while reducing cost, creating a better experience for both customers and associates."

Looking ahead, Williams-Sonoma (WSM) plans to use a proprietary AI platform to rapidly create secure business-specific applications and automate more workflows across functions such as finance, HR, and technology.

Shares of Williams-Sonoma (WSM) are up 38% since they bottomed out in April amid broad tariff concerns.
